coaching.coaching: Pin down acceptance criteria completeness and verifiability with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: acceptance criteria completeness and verifiability

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: etc.
verify command `grep` inspects output with `grep` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
verify command `ls` inspects output with `ls` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
verify command `grep` inspects output with `grep` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
verify command `grep` inspects output with `grep` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the plan title to remove the stale '~29 patch strings' claim and replace it with the accurate framing (e.g., 'migrate ~18 path-constant + ~18 dotted-import test targets atomically').
evidence: Title: 'migrate ~29 patch strings atomically'
Body: 'there are zero `mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…")` string patches'
findings.finding: The plan title ('migrate ~29 patch strings atomically') directly contradicts the body ('there are zero `mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…")` string patches'), creating a conflicting signal an executor reading only the title/subject would act on incorrectly.
findings.location: Title vs. Context section: 'the first draft's ~29 mock.patch strings was wrong; there are zero mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…") string patches'
scenarios: An executor reads the title and spends time searching for string-based patch sites that don't exist, or interprets category-2/3 counts differently.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename title to: 'Relocate acli*/adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits into adapters/jira/ + teach loader; rewrite ~18+18 category-2/3 test targets atomically'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete, anchored grep command to the structural gate that explicitly excludes only `patch.object` call sites, e.g.: `grep -rE 'rebar_reconciler\.(acli|adf|outbound_fields|comment_limits)' src/ tests/ | grep -v 'patch\.object'` — and embed this exact command in both the Testing section and AC line 3.
evidence: 'grep finds zero references to the old root paths of the moved modules in src/ and tests/ (excluding the path-agnostic `_load_acli` `patch.object` sites)'
No grep command or exclusion pattern is provided.
findings.finding: The structural gate's grep exclusion criterion ('excluding the path-agnostic `_load_acli` patches') is stated in prose but no concrete grep pattern is given, leaving the executor to guess a pattern that could silently include `patch.object` call sites or miss actual old-path string references (substring false-positive / false-negative risk per defect taxonomy item 1).
findings.location: Testing section — 'Structural gate' bullet; AC line 3
scenarios: A naive `grep -r 'rebar_reconciler.acli' tests/` matches `patch.object(applier, '_load_acli', ...)` strings and reports false hits, causing the gate to appear red when it should be green.
Conversely, an overly-broad exclusion pattern silently skips real old-path string references, making the gate appear green when stale references remain.
findings.suggested_fix: grep -rE 'rebar_reconciler\.(acli|adf|outbound_fields|comment_limits)' src/ tests/ | grep -v 'patch\.object' — assert output is empty.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly requires the 'teeth' check: e.g., 'Prior to the final commit, staging with one category-2 path constant left at the old root path causes at least one test to fail RED — confirmed by a local run and noted in the PR description.'
evidence: 'Teeth: leave one category-2 path constant at the old path and confirm that test errors.' — Testing section only
No AC requires this RED demonstration to be performed or recorded.
findings.finding: The 'teeth' falsifiability demonstration (leave one category-2 path constant at the old path, confirm RED) is described only in the Testing section and has no corresponding acceptance criterion, so a completing agent can skip it without violating any AC.
findings.location: Testing section — 'Teeth' bullet; Acceptance Criteria section (absence)
scenarios: An executor completes all five ACs (all green) without ever running the falsifiability check, so the gate's ability to actually catch a missed migration is never verified.
A future reader cannot tell from the AC list whether the teeth check was performed.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '- [ ] [operator-attested] Staging with one category-2 `importlib` path constant unrewritten produces a test failure (RED), confirming the structural gate has sensitivity — recorded in PR description with the failing test name.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief inline docstring or comment to `_loader.lazy_load` (and/or a note in AGENTS.md / ADR 0035) documenting that location-pinned modules in sub-packages must pass a relative subpath (e.g., `"adapters/jira/adf.py"`) rather than a bare filename, so the convention is discoverable without reading the implementation.
evidence: Plan states: '`lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, "adapters/jira/adf.py")` etc. — which resolves correctly and leaves all other root lookups untouched'
No mention of updating AGENTS.md, loader docstring, or ADR 0035 to document the new calling convention
ADR 0035 is cited only for the move decision, not for the loader sub-path pattern
findings.finding: The relative-subpath calling convention introduced for `lazy_load` (e.g., `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, "adapters/jira/adf.py")` vs. the existing `lazy_load(key, "filename.py")` root form) is a new internal loader contract. Any future developer adding a location-pinned module to a sub-package must know to pass a relative subpath rather than a bare filename — but the plan mentions no update to AGENTS.md, a loader docstring, or ADR 0035 to capture this convention.
scenarios: A future developer adds a new location-pinned module to a different sub-adapter and uses the bare-filename form, causing a silent resolution failure at runtime rather than at load time.
A reviewer reading `_loader.py` sees two different call shapes with no inline explanation of when to use which.
findings.suggested_fix: In `_loader.py`, add a docstring to `lazy_load` clarifying: 'Pass a bare filename for root-level modules (resolved against `_PACKAGE_DIR`) or a relative subpath (e.g., `adapters/jira/adf.py`) for modules in sub-packages.' Optionally add a one-liner to AGENTS.md under the loader section referencing this rule.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the plan title to accurately reflect the actual test-migration work (rewriting importlib path constants and dotted import statements, not mock.patch strings) now that the body has self-corrected.
evidence: Title: 'migrate ~29 patch strings atomically'
Body correction: 'there are zero mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…") string patches'
Actual test work: rewrite ~18 importlib path constants (category 2) and ~18 dotted imports (category 3) — neither category is a mock.patch string migration
findings.finding: The plan's title says 'migrate ~29 patch strings atomically' but the body explicitly states this characterization was wrong — there are zero mock.patch string patches. An implementer reading only the headline has an incorrect mental model of the test-migration work, which may cause confusion about scope or cause a reviewer to flag missing work.
findings.location: Plan title: 'migrate ~29 patch strings atomically'; Plan section 'Test surface': 'the first draft's "~29 mock.patch strings" was wrong; there are zero mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…") string patches'
scenarios: A reviewer or implementer reading only the title expects the bulk of the test work to involve rewriting mock.patch string arguments; instead the work is rewriting importlib path constants and dotted import statements — a different kind of edit requiring different search patterns.
findings.suggested_fix: Change title to: 'Relocate acli*/adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits into adapters/jira/ + teach loader; rewrite category-2/3 test path constants and imports atomically'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the category-2 migration enumeration to explicitly include non-SCRIPTS_DIR path-constant spellings for moved modules, specifically `test_adf.py:22` (`REPO_ROOT / ... / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'adf.py'`) and `test_reconcile_roundtrip.py:59` (`RECONCILER_DIR / 'adf.py'`), so the atomic edit-set covers all path-constant variants.
evidence: Plan category-2: '~18 files hardcode importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(…, "<moved>.py") / SCRIPTS_DIR/… "acli.py"-style path constants naming a moved module'
test_adf.py:22: ADF_PATH = REPO_ROOT / 'src' / 'rebar' / '_engine' / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'adf.py'
test_reconcile_roundtrip.py:59: return _load_module('rebar_reconciler.adf', RECONCILER_DIR / 'adf.py')
Neither uses SCRIPTS_DIR as the base, so they escape a SCRIPTS_DIR-pattern mechanical sweep
findings.finding: The plan's category-2 enumeration (path constants naming moved modules) characterizes the canonical `SCRIPTS_DIR / 'rebar_reconciler' / f'{moved}.py'` spelling but omits at least two test files that reference `adf.py` via structurally different base-path expressions: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_adf.py:22` uses `REPO_ROOT / 'src' / 'rebar' / '_engine' / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'adf.py'` and `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_roundtrip.py:59` uses `RECONCILER_DIR / 'adf.py'`. A mechanical pass over only the canonical SCRIPTS_DIR form will miss these, leaving broken path constants after the move.
scenarios: Implementer migrates all SCRIPTS_DIR-based path constants but leaves test_adf.py and test_reconcile_roundtrip.py pointing at the old root location, causing those tests to raise FileNotFoundError after the move despite the structural gate grep passing.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Edit-set or Testing section, add a note: 'category-2 also includes variant base-path spellings — grep for `/ "adf.py"` and `/ "comment_limits.py"` anywhere under tests/ to ensure RECONCILER_DIR and REPO_ROOT-rooted constants are updated alongside the SCRIPTS_DIR variants.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or correct the claim that `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` needs updating for `adf`/`outbound_fields`/`comment_limits` — grep confirms `__main__.py` never references those three modules; the loader edit sites are `outbound_fields.py`, `outbound_comments.py`, `inbound_translate.py`, `reconcile_check.py`, and `inbound_fields.py`.
evidence: Grep of __main__.py for 'adf' returns no matches
Grep of __main__.py for 'comment_limits' returns no matches
_load_sibling_keyed is called only at __main__.py:516 (mode.py) and __main__.py:534 (_advisory_lock.py)

The three location-pinned modules are loaded via lazy_load in outbound_fields.py:69, outbound_comments.py:84/100, inbound_translate.py:323, reconcile_check.py:70/75, inbound_fields.py:36 — none of these are in __main__.py
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three [adf, outbound_fields, comment_limits]' as part of the loader work, but `__main__.py` never references `adf`, `outbound_fields`, or `comment_limits` — `_load_sibling_keyed` is only called for `mode.py` and `_advisory_lock.py`. The work item does not exist and no `spec_from_file_location` site in `__main__.py` loads the three location-pinned modules.
findings.location: Approach section — 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three the same way'
scenarios: An implementer following the plan literally will search __main__.py for `_load_sibling_keyed` calls referencing the three modules and find nothing to update, creating confusion about whether the plan is correct or the code state is wrong
If the plan is interpreted as requiring a new `_load_sibling_keyed` call in __main__.py for these modules, spurious code changes could be introduced
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three the same way' with the actual edit sites: the `lazy_load(…, "adf.py")` calls in `outbound_fields.py`, `outbound_comments.py`, `inbound_translate.py`, and `reconcile_check.py`, plus the `Path(__file__).parent / "adf.py"` site in `inbound_fields.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the category-3 grep sweep (dotted-import targets) explicitly covers the `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/` subdirectory and test-root files, where at least 7 additional `from rebar_reconciler import acli*` / `from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import` usages exist that are not characterized in the plan's test-surface analysis.
evidence: grep 'from rebar_reconciler import acli' in tests/ yields: test_retry_httperror.py:113, test_config_file_wiring.py:21, test_comment_pagination.py:26-27, test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py:24-25, test_ev3c_env_renames.py:10
grep 'from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import' yields: test_config_jira_wiring.py:20
grep 'from rebar_reconciler.acli import' yields: test_error_taxonomy.py:25
Plan claims '~18 files use direct dotted imports' but hedge is 'grep-confirmed at S5 time'
findings.finding: Several test files use top-level dotted imports (`from rebar_reconciler import acli`, `from rebar_reconciler import acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess`, `from rebar_reconciler import acli_rest`, `from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import resolve_jira_settings`) — confirmed by grep. These are genuine category-3 targets that will break after the move, but the plan's stated scope for category-3 ('direct dotted imports `from rebar_reconciler.acli… / .adf / .outbound_fields`') does not explicitly name or count: `test_comment_pagination.py`, `test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py`, `test_config_file_wiring.py`, `test_ev3c_env_renames.py`, `test_retry_httperror.py`, `test_config_jira_wiring.py`, `test_error_taxonomy.py`. The plan's hedge ('grep-confirmed at S5 time') provides some cover, but the plan's description implies the only category-3 files are in the test subdirectories under `apply/`, `fetch/`, and `mutate/` — not in `state/` or `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/` root.
findings.location: Context section — 'Categories 2+3 (their deduped union) are the atomic migration set'; Test surface paragraph
scenarios: If the implementer assumes category-3 is contained to the apply/fetch/mutate subdirectories and skips grepping state/ and test root files, these 7 additional test files will still reference `rebar_reconciler.acli*` after the move and fail with ImportError
The AC structural gate ('grep finds zero references to the old root paths') would catch this — but only if the grep scope includes these files

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `jira_fields.py:19,22` (`from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import …`) to the enumerated repoint targets, alongside the already-listed line-18 adf import.
evidence: Plan states: 'adapters/jira/jira_fields.py:18 — from rebar_reconciler.adf import fit_text_to_adf_limit … → from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf import …'
jira_fields.py:19: from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import (_JIRA_COMMENT_MAX_CHARS,)
jira_fields.py:22: from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import (truncate_comment_body as _truncate_comment_body,)
These comment_limits imports at lines 19/22 are not mentioned in the plan's enumeration of jira_fields.py changes.
findings.finding: jira_fields.py imports comment_limits at lines 19 and 22 (`from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits import ...`) in addition to the adf import at line 18, but the plan only enumerates the line-18 adf reference as the repoint target for jira_fields.py.
findings.location: Context / Problem section, 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint' bullet for jira_fields.py:18; also Scope section
scenarios: After move, jira_fields.py (now at adapters/jira/) would still try to import from rebar_reconciler.comment_limits (root), which no longer exists there, causing ImportError.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the jira_fields.py repoint entry to: lines 18-22 — both `rebar_reconciler.adf` and `rebar_reconciler.comment_limits` imports → `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf` and `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.comment_limits` respectively.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `acli.py:76` (`from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf`) to the enumerated surviving intra-adapter references, alongside the already-listed `acli_cli_ops.py:36`.
evidence: Plan enumerates 'acli_cli_ops.py:36 — from rebar_reconciler.adf import … (moves with the acli* cluster; becomes an intra-adapter import)'
acli.py:76: from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf as _text_to_adf  # canonical location
acli.py moves to adapters/jira/ as part of the same cluster, so this import also becomes intra-adapter and needs the same fix.
findings.finding: acli.py:76 contains `from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf` — a dotted import identical in kind to acli_cli_ops.py:36 — but it is not enumerated in the plan's list of surviving intra-adapter references to repoint.
findings.location: Context / Problem section, 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint'; Scope section
scenarios: After both acli.py and adf.py move to adapters/jira/, acli.py:76 still references the old root path rebar_reconciler.adf, causing an ImportError at load time.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the edit list: 'acli.py:76 — from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf → from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf import text_to_adf (both files now co-located under adapters/jira/)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `outbound_fields.py:69` (`lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')`) to the enumerated edit-set, updating it to `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adapters/jira/adf.py')` after the move.
evidence: outbound_fields.py:69: _AdfModule = lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')
Plan enumerates the loader fix as: 'pass a relative subpath for the three moved location-pinned modules — lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, "adapters/jira/adf.py") etc.'
outbound_fields.py will move to adapters/jira/, but its internal lazy_load call for 'adf.py' is not listed as a site needing the relative-subpath fix.
findings.finding: outbound_fields.py:69 calls `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')` to load adf. After outbound_fields.py moves to adapters/jira/, this call still resolves relative to _PACKAGE_DIR (the root rebar_reconciler/ dir via _loader.py:39), but adf.py will no longer be there — this is exactly the loader-breakage pattern the plan describes for the three location-pinned modules, but outbound_fields.py's own internal lazy_load of adf is not in the enumerated edit-set.
findings.location: Context / Problem section, 'Loader' and 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references' bullets; Scope section
scenarios: After the move, outbound_fields.py (now at adapters/jira/) calls lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py'), which resolves to rebar_reconciler/adf.py (root) — a path that no longer exists — causing FileNotFoundError or ImportError at runtime.
findings.suggested_fix: In the moved adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py, change lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py') to lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adapters/jira/adf.py') so _loader.py resolves it relative to _PACKAGE_DIR (the root).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's loader-update instructions: remove the reference to `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` for the three location-pinned modules and replace it with the actual call sites (outbound_fields.py:69, outbound_comments.py:84/100, inbound_translate.py:323, reconcile_check.py:70/75, inbound_fields.py:36).
evidence: grep of __main__.py for 'adf', 'outbound_fields', 'comment_limits' returns zero hits
__main__._load_sibling_keyed is called only twice: for _MODE_KEY/'mode.py' and _ADVISORY_LOCK_KEY/'_advisory_lock.py'
The loader call sites for adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits are in outbound_fields.py:69, outbound_comments.py:84,100, inbound_translate.py:323, reconcile_check.py:70,75, inbound_fields.py:36
findings.finding: The plan states 'Update __main__._load_sibling_keyed and the importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...) sites for these three the same way' referring to adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits, but __main__.py has no _load_sibling_keyed or spec_from_file_location call sites for those three modules — it only calls _load_sibling_keyed for mode.py and _advisory_lock.py. The actual loader call sites for the three modules are in _loader.lazy_load callers (outbound_fields.py, outbound_comments.py, inbound_translate.py, reconcile_check.py, etc.).
findings.location: Context / Problem section, 'Loader' bullet: 'Update __main__._load_sibling_keyed and the importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...) sites for these three the same way.'
scenarios: Developer following the plan looks for __main__ call sites to update but finds none, creating confusion; the actual call sites that need updating may be missed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_jira_wiring.py:20`, `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_identity_264f_heldout.py:16`, and `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_error_taxonomy.py:25` to the enumerated category-3 test targets requiring dotted-import rewrite.

findings.finding: Several test files containing category-3 dotted imports from moved modules are unenumerated: test_config_jira_wiring.py:20 (`from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import resolve_jira_settings`), test_identity_264f_heldout.py:16 (`import rebar_reconciler.acli as acli`), and test_error_taxonomy.py:25 (`from rebar_reconciler.acli import RetryExhaustedError`). All three use the old root-module dotted path that must be updated to adapters.jira.*.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/scripts/bridge/test_jira_capability_probe.py:92` (path-string check `'rebar_reconciler/acli' in str(path)`) to the category-2 edit-set, updating it to match the new `adapters/jira/acli` path.
evidence: tests/scripts/bridge/test_jira_capability_probe.py:92: if 'rebar_reconciler/acli' in str(path) or name == 'acli_integration':
Plan describes category-2 as 'importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...) / SCRIPTS_DIR/… acli.py-style path constants naming a moved module → must be rewritten'
This path-string comparison inside a patched spec_from_file_location intercepts real loads of acli.py by path; post-move the path changes.
findings.finding: tests/scripts/bridge/test_jira_capability_probe.py:92 contains `if 'rebar_reconciler/acli' in str(path)` — a path-string check that matches the pre-move file path. After acli.py moves to adapters/jira/, this string won't match `'rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/acli'`, breaking the probe test's mock interception. This is a category-2 path-string consumer not enumerated in the plan.
findings.location: Context / Problem section, 'Test surface' category 2 description; Scope section
scenarios: After the move, the probe test's fake spec injection misses (path no longer contains 'rebar_reconciler/acli'), the real acli spec is used, and the test fails to inject the mock AcliClient.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the condition to: `if 'adapters/jira/acli' in str(path) or name == 'acli_integration':`

findings.finding: acli.py:76 also contains `from rebar_reconciler.adf import text_to_adf` (a dotted import of the module being moved), but the plan's explicit 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint' list names only `acli_cli_ops.py:36` for the intra-cluster adf import — omitting acli.py:76. After the move, both files land in adapters/jira/ and both imports must be repointed; missing one leaves a broken import on the moved module.

Plan's structural gate: 'grep finds zero references to the old root paths of the moved modules in src/ and tests/' — this gate would catch it but the approach section gives no fix
findings.finding: `inbound_probe.py` does `from rebar_reconciler import acli_subprocess` (line 65) and is absent from S4's file_impact list — meaning S4 does NOT rewire it. After acli_subprocess.py moves to adapters/jira/ in this story, that import path breaks. The plan's repoint enumeration does not mention inbound_probe.py, and the structural grep-gate ('zero references to old root paths') would catch this — but the plan gives implementers no guidance to fix it.
findings.location: Approach section — 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint'; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_probe.py:65
scenarios: After acli_subprocess.py moves to adapters/jira/, any code path through inbound_probe._resolve_env() raises ImportError: 'cannot import name acli_subprocess from rebar_reconciler'.
The structural grep gate catches it, but the implementer has no prescribed fix in the approach, risking confusion about whether to treat inbound_probe.py as in-scope for S4 or S5.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the '~29 test patch strings' characterization with the parent epic AC (bbf1-82e1-cf9d-494a): either amend the epic AC to reflect the corrected category-2/3 breakdown (and re-attest the epic's plan-review), then re-state the leaf's approach as a subset of the updated parent, or retain the original characterization if the count is still valid under a broader definition.
evidence: Leaf: 'the first draft's ~29 mock.patch strings was wrong; there are zero mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…") string patches'
Epic grandparent AC: 'all ~29 test patch strings are migrated with no re-export shims (S5)'

Parent (S4) description: '23 test files use that seam — they inject types.SimpleNamespace(AcliClient=lambda **_: mock_client)' — consistent with the patch.object characterization the leaf introduces, but neither S4 nor the epic AC pre-authorized the leaf to declare the ~29 figure obsolete without amending the parent.
findings.finding: The leaf unilaterally declares the parent/grandparent epic's '~29 test patch strings' figure 'wrong' and substitutes a different characterization (zero string patches; instead ~18 path-constant files + ~18 dotted-import files), contradicting the epic AC rather than updating the parent first.
findings.location: Leaf plan 'Test surface' section; Epic AC item: 'all ~29 test patch strings are migrated with no re-export shims (S5)'
scenarios: A reviewer reading the epic AC sees '~29 test patch strings' and checks for that migration; the leaf delivers something different in kind (path-constant rewrites + dotted-import rewrites) without the parent AC being updated to reflect this — creating a verification mismatch.

A future auditor comparing the epic's signed AC against S5's delivered work finds a stated deliverable ('~29 string patches migrated') that the leaf claims does not exist, with no parent amendment on record.
findings.suggested_fix: Update epic AC (bbf1-82e1-cf9d-494a) to read something like: 'All surviving test references to the old root paths of the moved modules — path-constant strings (~18 files) and dotted-import strings (~18 files) — are rewritten to adapters.jira.* paths, with no re-export shims.' Then re-state S5's test-surface section as a subset of that updated language, dropping the 'the first draft was wrong' framing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the grep sweep spec to explicitly cover: (a) `tests/_engine_path.py::acli_path()` and all its callers, (b) `tests/scripts/conftest.py`'s independent hardcoded `ACLI_PATH`, and (c) path-string references to moved modules outside `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/` (e.g., `tests/interfaces/` and `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py`) — confirm all are in the category-2 rewrite set or explicitly noted as inert.
evidence: Plan: 'the exact surviving reference set is re-confirmed by grep at implementation time' — defers enumeration without bounding the search scope

Plan names only three exemplar dotted-import sites (jira_fields.py:18, inbound_fields.py:36, acli_cli_ops.py:36) and characterizes ~18+18 test targets but does not account for the shared path-helper cluster
tests/_engine_path.py:25-26: `def acli_path() -> Path: return engine_dir() / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'acli.py'` — a structural helper not mentioned
tests/scripts/conftest.py:37: `ACLI_PATH = REPO_ROOT / 'src' / 'rebar' / '_engine' / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'acli.py'` — a separate hardcoded constant not covered by the SCRIPTS_DIR pattern
tests/interfaces/store/test_commits_on_ticket.py:119: `/ 'outbound_fields.py'` — hardcoded path read-as-text in a non-reconciler-unit test tree not covered by the category-2 enumeration

tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py:193: `_load_attr('outbound_fields.py', '_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_STATUS')` using a helper that builds `REPO_ROOT / 'src' / 'rebar' / '_engine' / 'rebar_reconciler' / module_file`
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the deduped category-2/3 test targets' constitute the complete atomic migration set, but the concrete grep sweep it defers to implementation leaves at least two cross-cutting path-constant clusters unaccounted for: (1) `tests/_engine_path.py::acli_path()` (returns `engine_dir() / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'acli.py'`) consumed by `tests/scripts/conftest.py`; and (2) `tests/interfaces/store/test_commits_on_ticket.py:119` and `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config.py:193`, both of which hardcode `outbound_fields.py` as a path string built against the engine root. These are not named as examples, are not covered by the 11 `ACLI_PATH` / 7 `_ADF_PATH` / 7 `_CL_PATH` `SCRIPTS_DIR`-relative constants the plan describes, and would break silently after the move if missed.
findings.location: Context / Problem — 'Edit-set … Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references' and Testing — 'Structural gate: grep finds zero references'
scenarios: After the move, `tests/scripts/conftest.py` loads `acli.py` from the old root path via `acli_path()` → `FileNotFoundError`; the plan's structural grep wouldn't catch this unless it explicitly searches for `_engine_path` usages.

After the move, `test_commits_not_a_jira_synced_field` reads `outbound_fields.py` by absolute path from the old location → `FileNotFoundError` or silently reads nothing, making the assertion vacuously pass.
After the move, `test_idea_maps_to_jira_idea_across_all_status_maps` loads `outbound_fields.py` via `_load_attr('outbound_fields.py', ...)` → `FileNotFoundError`.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'The grep sweep must include (i) `tests/_engine_path.py` and its consumers, (ii) all `tests/scripts/` path constants, and (iii) any path-string reference to moved modules outside the reconciler unit-test subtree (e.g., `tests/interfaces/` and cross-cutting `test_config.py` parity assertions). These are category-2 targets and must be rewritten to the `adapters/jira/` paths in the same atomic change.'
